model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"With only 256 optimizer steps, regularization is likely dominating and slowing fit. Disabling LR decay and removing weight decay should improve early optimization speed and reduce underfitting in this tight budget."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size exposes the model to more examples within the same 256 updates, which often improves validation loss in short-budget runs without altering optimization logic."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The run is still likely capacity-limited under short training. Increasing channel width and classifier hidden size should improve representational power while keeping the architecture simple and stable."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","new":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),\n            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The model is still improving strongly from added capacity, so adding one more residual block is a targeted depth increase likely to improve feature refinement within the fixed training budget."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"After increasing depth and width, optimization may be slightly underpowered at the previous learning rate. A moderate LR increase should improve progress within the same 256-step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The higher learning rate degraded validation loss, so reverting to the previously better value restores the stronger optimization setting for this architecture."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"A small amount of label smoothing can improve generalization for a higher-capacity model trained with a short budget, often reducing validation loss without major optimization changes."}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Try decoupled weight decay with AdamW while removing label smoothing that slightly hurt performance; this can improve generalization for the larger model without changing training-loop complexity."}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Cosine scheduling significantly hurt this setup, so disabling the schedule restores the stronger constant-LR regime used by the current best configuration."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"In fixed-step mode, increasing batch size can improve gradient quality and doubles examples seen per optimizer step, which may further reduce validation loss for this stable AdamW setup."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Batch size 256 regressed and increased runtime substantially; reverting to 128 returns the better compute/optimization tradeoff for this fixed-step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The model may still be slightly over-regularized for a 256-step budget; reducing AdamW weight decay can improve fit while keeping the optimizer and architecture unchanged."}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Restore the better weight decay value and test a lower AdamW beta2 to adapt second-moment estimates faster in a short 256-step regime, which can improve optimization speed."}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Lower beta2 degraded performance; restoring the previous beta setting returns the more stable second-moment averaging that matched the best loss so far."}

            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Capacity scaling has been consistently helpful so far; adding one more residual block is a minimal depth extension that may further improve representation quality within the same training setup."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"With the deeper 3-block model now performing best, a modest learning-rate increase may improve optimization progress within the same fixed 256-step budget."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"With the deeper backbone now strongest, increasing classifier width is a small targeted capacity boost that may improve class separation without altering optimization settings."}
